sections.question_synthetic: The sender is requesting written confirmation from the FPPC Sacramento office regarding volunteer reimbursement rules (up to $100 within 45 days) and petty cash fund procedures (up to $100 for bills not exceeding $100) to address campaign payment issues.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No formal question or conclusion sections present. The sender references a prior phone conversation with someone named Wayne and requests written confirmation of guidance already provided verbally. Document appears to be the outgoing letter from the campaign, not the FPPC's response letter.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The sender is requesting written confirmation from the FPPC Sacramento office regarding volunteer reimbursement rules (up to $100 within 45 days) and petty cash fund procedures (up to $100 for bills not exceeding $100) to address campaign payment issues.
embedding.first_500_words: Dear Wayne, As per our phone conversation this A.M., I have had problems on paying small bills of the campaign. You said volunteers can be reimbursed up to $100 within 45 days, or a petty cash fund of $100 for bills not exceeding $100. A letter from the Sacramento office stating those two rules will solve our problems. In campaigns for either party with 3570 registrations, is not going to draw huge campaign funds or hire treasurers or campaign Dirs. But, volunteers are hard enough to come by, without being hassled about trivial matters. I don't know, or care, what problems exist with the local contact: such a letter will square two separate hassles: and many campaigns have busy people as treasurer and candidate that can't sign two party checks on a day's notice for less than $100. Thank you for responding to our needs. Yours Truly, Duane K. Brown The image you provided is completely white, with no visible content or text. If you have another page or section of the document that you would like to analyze or transcribe, please provide it.
embedding.summary: This is an incoming letter from a campaign representative requesting formal FPPC confirmation of volunteer reimbursement and petty cash procedures to address administrative challenges in managing small campaign expenses.
